commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a cron-service callback for pre-runner isolated-agent setup timeouts, wires gateway cron to request a safe gateway restart, and adds cron/gateway regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +59, Tests +97. Total +156 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main emits the pre-runner isolated setup-timeout class and has no gateway restart callback for it. I did not run a live current-main repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the automatic restart policy plus contributor-supplied PR-head proof, not a narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to attempt.

target.title: fix: restart gateway after isolated cron setup timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- classify isolated cron setup timeouts as a gateway-health failure class
- notify the gateway cron service when setup stalls before the isolated runner starts
- request a safe gateway restart through the existing restart coordinator so affected recurring jobs can recover centrally
- add cron-service and gateway regression coverage for the restart path

## Root Cause
Cron jobs that use isolated agent turns can fail before the runner starts with `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start`. Today that error is recorded and alerted, but it is not treated as a platform-level gateway/isolated-runner health failure. Individual cron workflows can self-repair around it, but the shared failure mode keeps recurring across unrelated jobs.

## Impact
When this failure class occurs, OpenClaw now asks the existing safe-restart coordinator to restart only after the normal preflight drain says it is safe. This should reduce repeated user-visible cron failure cards without interrupting active work.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Recurring isolated-agent cron jobs were repeatedly failing before runner start while the gateway was stale/hot, producing `cron: isolated agent setup timed out before runner start` cards instead of recovering centrally.
- **Real environment tested:** Real macOS OpenClaw setup on Ghita's machine, LaunchAgent-managed local gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:18789`, OpenClaw `2026.5.28`, Telegram/Clio plus YC YouTube recurring ingest workload active.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** I applied the same recovery behavior this PR automates by using OpenClaw's normal safe update/restart path, then ran live checks: `openclaw gateway status`, `openclaw status --json`, and `ps -p 93467 -o pid,pcpu,pmem,rss,etime,command`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the live setup showed `openclaw gateway status` with `Runtime: running (pid 93467, state active)`, `Connectivity probe: ok`, `Capability: admin-capable`, and `Gateway version: 2026.5.28`. Copied live output from `openclaw status --json` showed `gateway.reachable=true`, `gateway.connectLatencyMs=45`, `queuedSystemEvents=[]`, and `tasks.byStatus.queued=0`. Copied live `ps` output showed gateway pid `93467` at about `1.0%` CPU, `3.2%` memory, RSS `534560` KB. Before recovery, the same setup had a stale gateway timeout and a gateway node process around `109%` CPU with roughly `2.8GB` RSS.
- **Observed result after fix:** The gateway recovered and stayed reachable instead of continuing the setup-timeout loop. Normal OpenClaw work resumed through the restored gateway: the YC YouTube recurring ingest was running with active `youtube_channel_recurring_ingest.py`, `media_mirror_url_batch.py`, `media_mirror_ingest.py`, and `extract_import_insights.py` processes, rather than being blocked by the stale-gateway/setup-timeout failure class.
- **What was not tested:** The exact PR build was not installed into the live machine; the live proof verifies the real operational recovery path this PR wires into the cron setup-timeout handler. Automated regression coverage verifies the new handler calls `requestSafeGatewayRestart({ reason: "cron.isolated_agent_setup_timeout", delayMs: 0 })` for the setup-timeout class.

## Testing
- `pnpm test src/cron/service/timer.regression.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `pnpm check:changed` was also attempted locally before this PR, but the local Blacksmith Testbox wrapper failed its own `--version/--help` sanity check before project checks could run.
